scope.area: installer, localhost smoke, uninstall, purge, and cleanup timing
coverage: runtime boot and localhost smoke validation
purge timing and final residue cleanup

summary.highLevelVerdict: The true installer clone flow, scaffold creation, uninstall, and purge all completed on March 23, 2026, but the localhost validation phase failed because the runtime manager user could not read the scaffold host config under /var/opt/ehecoatl/tenants/localhost/www/config.json. Cleanup also exposed two residue issues: uninstall left /etc/opt/ehecoatl/install-meta.env behind despite logging its removal, and purge left a stopped root PM2 entry that required one manual sudo pm2 delete Ehecoatl step.

commands.installer: EHECOATL_REPO_URL=<temporary bare snapshot of the current working tree> ./installer.sh

details: The lifecycle used a temporary standalone installer directory so installer.sh followed the true clone-to-/opt/ehecoatl path instead of reusing the authoring checkout.
A second checkout remained available for purge-data.sh after uninstall removed /opt/ehecoatl.
Pre-run checks confirmed no pre-existing /opt/ehecoatl, /etc/opt/ehecoatl, /var/opt/ehecoatl, /srv/opt/ehecoatl, or root PM2 entries.
installer.sh completed the true clone flow into /opt/ehecoatl and handed off to setup/setup.sh.
The localhost scaffold prompt was answered with localhost and setup reported that www.localhost was created.
The CLI symlink pointed at /opt/ehecoatl/cli/ehecoatl after setup, and runtime data trees were created under /etc/opt/ehecoatl, /var/opt/ehecoatl, and /srv/opt/ehecoatl.
sudo npm run start returned successfully and root PM2 launched two Ehecoatl instances immediately.

No successful localhost response was observed within the bounded 60 second poll window, so no trustworthy route-latency samples were captured for /, /htm/index.htm, /htm/cached.htm, /hello, or /session.
A direct stop completed cleanly in about 2.4 seconds and post-stop localhost requests failed with connection-refused as expected.

blocker.symptom: The manager process crash-looped instead of serving the scaffold tenant.
blocker.supportingObservation: The host directory and host config file had only owner/group permissions and no named ACL entry for e_manager, so the runtime manager user could not traverse/read the scaffold host config.

details: The uninstall script removed /opt/ehecoatl and the /usr/local/bin/ehecoatl symlink.
The expected preserved data trees under /etc/opt/ehecoatl, /var/opt/ehecoatl, and /srv/opt/ehecoatl remained after uninstall, which matches the documented preservation boundary.
Uninstall logged that installation metadata was removed, but /etc/opt/ehecoatl/install-meta.env was still present until purge removed the whole /etc/opt/ehecoatl tree.
After purge, the root PM2 process list still retained a stopped Ehecoatl entry even though the filesystem was clean.
One manual sudo pm2 delete Ehecoatl step was required to finish with no residual PM2 entry.

requiredChecks.item: A disposable execution target is chosen so uninstall does not destroy the authoring workspace
requiredChecks.note: A standalone installer directory plus a separate authoring checkout were used.

requiredChecks.item: Installer or setup completes and scaffolds the tenant-minimal host for www.localhost
requiredChecks.note: Setup reported successful localhost scaffold creation.

requiredChecks.item: Localhost access proves GET /, GET /htm/index.htm, GET /htm/cached.htm, GET /hello, and GET /session
requiredChecks.note: No successful localhost response was observed because the manager process could not read the scaffold host config.

requiredChecks.item: Runtime stop completes without hanging or supervised-child teardown drift
requiredChecks.note: A direct stop completed in 2443 ms and left the PM2 app in stopped state.

requiredChecks.item: Uninstall removes binaries, project files, and install metadata while preserving runtime data
requiredChecks.note: Project files and CLI were removed and data was preserved, but install-meta.env remained.

requiredChecks.item: Purge removes the preserved /etc/opt/ehecoatl, /var/opt/ehecoatl, and /srv/opt/ehecoatl trees
No residual PM2 process entry remains from the tested run
requiredChecks.note: Not after purge alone. One manual sudo pm2 delete Ehecoatl step was required.

requiredChecks.item: A report/performance artifact is written for install, smoke, stop, uninstall, and purge timings
requiredChecks.note: This report is the matching lifecycle artifact.

anomalies: The runtime never produced a first successful localhost response within the bounded 60 second smoke window.
Root PM2 logs showed the manager crash-looping on EACCES for /var/opt/ehecoatl/tenants/localhost/www/config.json.
The scaffold host directory and config file lacked a named ACL entry for e_manager, which matches the observed runtime blocker.
setup/uninstall.sh logged installation-metadata removal, but /etc/opt/ehecoatl/install-meta.env still existed until purge removed /etc/opt/ehecoatl.
purge-data.sh removed the data trees but did not clear the stopped root PM2 Ehecoatl entry.

nextAction.failedPhase: Validate Localhost Smoke Routes And Safe Stop
nextAction.observedSymptom: Manager crash-loop on localhost scaffold access with EACCES against the host config file
recommendedFixes: Fix scaffold ACL application so the manager runtime user can traverse the tenant host directory and read the host config file before booting localhost smoke checks.
Fix uninstall so its installation-metadata removal log matches reality.
Decide whether uninstall or purge should also clear the root PM2 entry, or document the required manual pm2 delete cleanup.
nextAction.followUpDisposition: The installer-localhost-uninstall lifecycle is not validated for this March 23, 2026 revision until the localhost ACL blocker is fixed and the cleanup residue behavior is clarified.
